expectations.text: Uses aws-iam auth type (not universal-auth with client-id/secret)
expectations.evidence: Discusses 'INFISICAL_AGENT_MODE: kubernetes' and machine identity with client credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager, not aws-iam auth type

expectations.text: Shows correct auth config with identity-id field
expectations.evidence: Shows 'secrets' with INFISICAL_CLIENT_ID/SECRET from AWS Secrets Manager, not identity-id field for aws-iam

expectations.text: Shows ECS task definition pattern with shared volume between agent and app containers
expectations.evidence: Task definition shows two containers (infisical-agent and my-app) with shared volume 'infisical-socket' mounted in both

expectations.text: Uses correct template function (listSecrets or listSecretsByProjectSlug) with proper signature
expectations.evidence: Does not show actual template with listSecrets or listSecretsByProjectSlug. Shows environment variable approach instead

expectations.text: Mentions exit-after-auth option for init-style sidecar pattern
expectations.evidence: Does not mention exit-after-auth option. Shows container running continuously

expectations.text: Does not require manually managing any credentials or access keys
expectations.evidence: Requires storing machine identity credentials in AWS Secrets Manager and managing them manually
